sections.paragraph: ἅμα [ἁ-copul. ‘together’ = Skt. sa-, cp. εἷς and oblique forms] adv. primarily signifying simultaneity ‘at once’; temporal aspect: of simultaneous actions at the same time Ac 24:26; Phlm 22; cp. 1 Th 5:10 (the point being that no matter what the circumstances, it makes no difference, the desired outcome will take place at the same time); of pers. acting simultaneously together Ro 3:12; of simultaneous effect Mt 13:29; 1 Th 4:17. W. adv. of time πρωΐ early in the morning, at the crack of dawn Mt 20:1.
